get.description: Get a paginated result for media based on the applied filters

parameters.name: post_id
parameters.description: Filter by post IDs. Multiple values imply OR logic (e.g., ?post_id=123&post_id=456).

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter by media types. Multiple values imply OR logic (e.g., ?type=image&type=video).

next.description: URL to the next page of results, or null if none.

500.description: Internal server error when fetching media.

404.description: Media not found based on the given ID.

500.description: Internal server error when fetching the media.

404.description: Media not found based on the given ID.

500.description: Internal server error when deleting the media.

200.description: Signed upload URL and media record created successfully.

get.description: Get a paginated result for posts based on the applied filters

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: Filter by platforms. Multiple values imply OR logic.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by post status. Multiple values imply OR logic.

next.description: URL to the next page of results, or null if none.

500.description: Internal server error when fetching posts.
Internal server error when fetching the Post.

404.description: Post not found based on the given ID.

500.description: Internal server error when fetching the Post.

patch.description: Update an existing post with the passed in data. If updating a 'scheduled' post make sure to always pass 'scheduled_at' otherwise the post will process immediately

500.description: Internal server error when updating the Post.
Internal server error when deleting the Post.

get.description: Get a paginated result for post results based on the applied filters

parameters.name: post_id
parameters.description: Filter by post IDs. Multiple values imply OR logic (e.g., ?post_id=123&post_id=456).

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: Filter by platform(s). Multiple values imply OR logic (e.g., ?platform=twitter&platform=facebook).

200.description: Paginated data set for post results.

next.description: URL to the next page of results, or null if none.

500.description: Internal server error when fetching post results.

404.description: Post result not found based on the given ID.

500.description: Internal server error when fetching the post result.

get.description: Get a paginated result for social accounts based on the applied filters

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: Filter by platform(s). Multiple values imply OR logic (e.g., ?platform=twitter&platform=instagram).

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Filter by username(s). Multiple values imply OR logic (e.g., ?username=test&username=test2).

200.description: Paginated data set for social accounts.

next.description: URL to the next page of results, or null if none.

500.description: Internal server error when fetching social accounts.

404.description: Social account not found based on the given ID.

500.description: Internal server error when fetching the post result.

tags.name: Auth
tags.description:
Authentication is required for all endpoints. Provide a valid API key as a Bearer token in the Authorization header. 
      Log in to your [post bridge](https://www.post-bridge.com/) account to retrieve your API key.

tags.name: Getting Started
Media
tags.description:
Media are media records (images, videos, etc.) that can be attached to posts. A Media record must be created before uploading the actual asset.
Media assets are stored temporarily and are automatically deleted in the following scenarios:
- When the associated post is published
- After 24 hours if not attached to any post
- When the scheduled post is deleted

These endpoints support common media operations like upload, retrieval, and deletion while ensuring efficient storage management.

tags.name: Posts
tags.description:
Posts represent content that can be published across multiple social media platforms. Each post can have platform-specific content variations, allowing customization for different platforms and accounts. Content can be defined at three levels:

1. Default content for all platforms
2. Platform-specific content overrides
3. Account-specific content overrides

The system will use the most specific content override available when publishing to each platform and account.

tags.name: Social Accounts
tags.description:
Social accounts represent platform-specific accounts (e.g. Twitter, LinkedIn, Facebook) that are used for publishing posts. 
Each social account has a unique `id` that can be referenced when creating or scheduling posts to specify which platforms the content should be published to.

tags.name: Post Results
tags.description:
Post results represent the outcome of publishing content to various social media platforms. They provide comprehensive information including:
- Publication status (success/failure)
- Any errors or issues encountered during posting
- Platform url to view the published post

isDeleted.description: The current state of the physical media asset

url.description: The URL of the media object

size_bytes.description: The size of the media object in bytes

name.description: The provided name of the media object

object.description: The media object represents the actual media asset

name.description: The original name of the file (for extension)

upload_url.description: The signed upload URL for the client to upload the file

name.description: The provided name of the media file

status.description: Current status of the post: posted, scheduled, or processing

scheduled_at.description: Scheduled date and time for the post

social_accounts.description: Array of social account IDs associated with the post

media.description: Array of media URLs associated with the post

created_at.description: Timestamp when the post was created

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the post was last updated

is_draft.description: If true the post will not be processed until updated

is_trial_reel.description: Enable trial reel mode. Shows reel to non-followers first. Requires: Professional/Creator account, 1,000+ followers, public profile. Max 5 trial reels per day.

trial_graduation.description: Trial graduation strategy. "MANUAL" (default) = you decide whether to share with all followers. "SS_PERFORMANCE" = auto-graduate based on performance within 72 hours.

draft.description: If true the post will be saved as a draft, instead of immediately publishing

is_aigc.description: If true the video will be labeled with "Creator labeled as AI-generated" tag in the description.

caption.description: Caption to user for the specified account

media.description: Array of Media Ids to use for the specifed account

account_configurations.description: List of account configurations to override data for specific accounts

scheduled_at.description: Scheduled date and time for the post. Setting to null or undefined will post instantly.

media.description: Array of media IDs associated with the post

media_urls.description: Array of publicly accesible media URLs associated with the post, will be ignored if media is provided

social_accounts.description: Array of social account IDs for posting

is_draft.description: If true will create the post but not process it until it is updated with a scheduled date or processed instantly

processing_enabled.description: If true will process video files to ensure they post, If false we will skip all video processing

is_draft.description: If true will create the post but not process it until it is updated with a scheduled date or processed instantly

processing_enabled.description: If true will process video files to ensure they post, If false we will skip all video processing

post_id.description: The ID of the associated post
